column1: INSTRUCTIONS: Chỉ ghi CORE PROMPTS (Decision/Problem-Solving/Verification). Mỗi entry phải trả lời đầy đủ 4 câu hỏi trong Human Delta.

column2: Prompt Type
DECISION
column4: Implementing a student ID search (exact match)
column5: "I have a list of 5,000 students. Should I use a Binary Search Tree or a HashMap for O(1) search?"
column6: AI suggested HashMap for O(1) average time complexity.
column7: Critical Thinking: While HashMap is O(1), it doesn't support ordered traversal. Contextualization: The project also requires printing a student list sorted by ID. Creative Synthesis: I decided to use a TreeMap (Red-Black Tree). Decision: Even though lookup is O(log n), it maintains order, which saves O(n log n) sorting time later.
column8: Comparison table of search vs. sort time

column2: PROBLEM-SOLVING
column4: Deleting a node from a Singly Linked List
column5: "How to delete a node in a Singly Linked List if I only have a pointer to that node?"
column6: AI suggested copying the data from the next node to the current node and deleting the next one.
column7: Critical Thinking: This trick fails if the node to be deleted is the Last Node. Contextualization: My list frequently deletes tail nodes. Decision Ownership: I modified the logic to handle the tail case by traversing from the head, ensuring no memory leaks.
column8: Code snippet highlighting the tail case fix

column2: VERIFICATION
column4: Checking if a BST is balanced
column5: "Provide an algorithm to check if a Binary Tree is height-balanced."
column6: AI provided a top-down recursion that calls height() on every node.
column7: Critical Thinking: This approach is O(n^2) because it recalculates height repeatedly. Creative Synthesis: I implemented a bottom-up approach that checks balance and height in one pass. Decision: Improved complexity from O(n^2) to O(n).
column8: Big O analysis and execution time log
